qa_pairs.question: What is the definition of an 'Applicant' in the context of the Resource Allocation Competition (RAC)?
qa_pairs.answer: An 'Applicant' is defined as the Principal Investigator (PI) and any co-PI listed in an application submitted to the Resource Allocation Competition (RAC).

qa_pairs.question: What commitment do individuals make by signing the confidentiality agreement?
qa_pairs.answer: By signing the agreement, individuals commit to treating all information received for evaluating resource applications as strictly confidential. This includes unpublished material, deliberations, comments, scores, and recommendations from the peer review committees.

qa_pairs.question: For what purpose can confidential information received by reviewers be used?
qa_pairs.answer: Confidential information received by reviewers must only be used for the purpose of evaluating resource applications and for no other purpose beyond that for which it was originally intended.

qa_pairs.question: What types of information must be kept confidential by reviewers during the Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) review process?
qa_pairs.answer: Reviewers must treat as strictly confidential all information received for evaluating resource applications, all unpublished material from the documents during the review process, and all deliberations, comments, scores, and recommendations of the peer review committees.
